name: Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria
region_intro: Mountain biking around Feria, Spain, offers diverse terrain on the southern slope of Sierra Vieja. The region features varied landscapes, including the oak forests known as Dehesas, which provide unique cycling environments. Riders can explore areas around Sierra del Molino and discover routes that incorporate local reservoirs and historic landmarks. The natural features of the area make it suitable for a range of mountain bike trails near Feria. Best mountain bike trails around Feria The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria, a 16.5 miles (26.5 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes of the Sierra del Pico and river areas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra, a moderate 32.7 miles (52.7 km) path. This route features scenic views around the Villalba de los Barros reservoir and passes by the Hermitage of San Juan. Local mountain bikers also love the Feria desde Fuente del Maestre — circular por el Embalse de Villalba de los Barros, a 23.3 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through the countryside around the Villalba de los Barros reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Feria is defined by the Sierra Vieja hills, open Dehesas, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Feria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Feria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Feria?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Feria, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 9 easy routes, 36 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Feria?
A: Mountain biking around Feria features diverse terrain, including the southern slope of Sierra Vieja, the rocky features of Sierra del Molino, and the unique oak forests known as Dehesas. You'll find a mix of hilly landscapes, open woodlands, and routes that incorporate local reservoirs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Feria?
A: Yes, many routes around Feria are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033566814/sierra-del-pico-rivera-del-guadajira-y-sierra-caliza-circular-desde-feria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 26.5 km loop. Another option is the 52.7 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030775861/embalse-de-villalba-de-los-barros-y-ermita-de-san-juan-circular-desde-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around the reservoir.

Q: What are the best routes for beginner mountain bikers in Feria?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are 9 easy mountain bike trails available around Feria. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother paths, making them suitable for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Feria?
A: The mountain bike trails in Feria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Nearly 100 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic reservoirs, open Dehesas, and the challenging yet rewarding Sierra Vieja hills.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or offer views of historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030775861/embalse-de-villalba-de-los-barros-y-ermita-de-san-juan-circular-desde-la-parra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find routes that offer views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent 15th-century landmark.

Q: Can I find routes that include reservoirs or water features?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic reservoirs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277537935/feria-desde-fuente-del-maestre-circular-por-el-embalse-de-villalba-de-los-barros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feria desde Fuente del Maestre — circular por el Embalse de Villalba de los Barros&lt;/a&gt; lead through the countryside around the Villalba de los Barros reservoir, providing beautiful waterside views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Feria?
A: Feria, located in Extremadura, Spain, generally experiences mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering comfortable temperatures and vibrant landscapes. It's advisable to check local weather conditions, especially during summer when temperatures can be very high.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Feria provides several opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, situated on an elevated position, offers impressive vistas of the surrounding Sierra Vieja and dehesas, and some routes will incorporate these viewpoints.

Q: What are some other attractions to explore near the mountain bike trails in Feria?
A: Beyond the trails, Feria's historic quarter is a National Historic Site with traditional whitewashed houses. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6458193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande in Zafra&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3761785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of La Candelaria&lt;/a&gt;, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;. For castle enthusiasts, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also within reach.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult mountain bike routes around Feria. These trails typically feature steeper climbs, more technical descents, and rugged terrain, providing a demanding experience for advanced mountain bikers.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike routes in Feria?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly. Moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033566814/sierra-del-pico-rivera-del-guadajira-y-sierra-caliza-circular-desde-feria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Pico, Rivera del Guadajira y Sierra Caliza – circular desde Feria&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 35 minutes, while longer routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Viejo de Villafranca – Camino de Los Santos loop from Fuente del Maestre&lt;/a&gt; might take over 4 hours 30 minutes to complete.
tips:
Arriving at Feria from La Parra along the road to the Port, you can see Feria, its castle (https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Castillo_de_Feria), Sierra Vieja, the Villalba reservoir and the entire plain of Tierra de Barros and in On days with good visibility you can see the Sierras de Piedras Blancas (Hornachos and Alange) and the Arroyo de S. Serván.
 



Very broken path with many loose stones. The return to La Parra through the Purgatorio area, going down to Arroyo de Valdelazarza, is complicated (broken path, with many loose stones and a steep slope).
Views of the valley and transition area to numerous routes through the Extremadura pasture and connection with the entire Sierra Southeast
Beautiful place in Extremadura next to the flow of the Guadajira river for lovers of mountain biking.
